title: Job Openings at Tonia Pharmacy Limited
content:
Tonia Pharmacy Limited was incorporated in the year 2003 (RC: 497235) to carry on business as a pharmaceutical company. Since inception, the company has grown to become one of the reputable pharmacies in Nigeria, with several branches located in various parts of the country.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Cashier

Job Location: All Branches, Nigeria
Department: Finance / Retail Operations
Reports To: Branch Manager / Superintendent Pharmacist.
Organization: Tonia Pharmacy Limited & Superstore

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Cashier is responsible for handling all financial transactions within the pharmacy and superstore in a timely, accurate, and professional manner.
	The role involves receiving payments, issuing receipts, maintaining cash accountability, and ensuring excellent customer service while complying with company policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities
Transaction Processing:


	Receive and process payments (cash, POS, transfers) for goods and services.
	Accurately issue receipts and invoices to customers.
	Ensure correct pricing, discounts, and promotions are applied.
	Balance cash register at the beginning and end of each shift.


Customer Service:


	Greet customers warmly and attend to them in a professional manner.
	Respond to inquiries regarding products, prices, and store policies.
	Handle customer complaints politely and escalate when necessary.
	Maintain a friendly and welcoming checkout environment.


Cash Handling & Accountability:


	Maintain proper custody of cash and prevent shortages or overages.
	Perform daily cash reconciliation and report discrepancies immediately.
	Ensure all transactions are properly recorded in the system.
	Safeguard POS machines and other financial tools.


Sales Support:


	Support sales activities by promoting ongoing offers and products.
	Cross-sell and upsell pharmacy and supermarket items where appropriate.
	Assist customers in locating products when necessary.


Record Keeping & Reporting:


	Maintain accurate transaction records and daily sales reports.
	Prepare end-of-day financial summaries.
	Assist in stock documentation when required.


Compliance & Controls:


	Adhere strictly to company financial policies and procedures.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and regulatory standards.
	Prevent fraud, theft, or misuse of company resources.


Store Operations Support:


	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the cashier area.
	Assist in stock arrangement and shelf monitoring when required.
	Support inventory counts and audits when assigned.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy in transactions (zero or minimal errors)
	Daily cash balancing compliance
	Customer satisfaction ratings
	Speed and efficiency of service
	Reduction in cash discrepancies
	Upselling contribution to sales.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc Degree (additional qualifications are an advantage)
	Proven experience as a cashier or in a retail environment (pharmacy experience is an added advantage)
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles
	Familiarity with POS systems and basic computer skills
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	High level of integrity and honesty
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to work under pressure
	Good customer service orientation
	Time management and multitasking abilities.


Working Conditions:


	Requires standing for long periods
	May involve weekend and shift work
	Fast-paced retail and healthcare environment.


Additional Requirements:


	Professional appearance and conduct
	Ability to handle confidential financial information
	Team player with a positive attitude.


Application Closing Date: 30th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Inventory Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 


	The Inventory Officer is responsible for the efficient management, control, and monitoring of stock within the pharmacy and store.
	This includes ensuring accurate stock levels, proper storage of pharmaceutical products, minimizing wastage/expiry, and maintaining compliance with regulatory standards.
	The role ensures uninterrupted availability of medicines and health products while maintaining accountability and transparency in inventory records.


Key Responsibilities 
Inventory Management & Control:


	Maintain accurate records of all stock received, stored, and issued using inventory systems.
	Monitor stock levels and ensure timely replenishment to avoid stock-outs.
	Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly stock counts (cycle counts and full stock-taking).
	Reconcile physical stock with system records and investigate discrepancies.
	Implement FIFO (First In, First Out) and FEFO (First Expiry, First Out) principles.


Receiving & Inspection of Goods:


	Verify incoming supplies against purchase orders and delivery notes.
	Inspect products for quality, quantity, batch numbers, and expiry dates.
	Ensure all received items meet regulatory and company standards.
	Record and report damaged, expired, or short-supplied goods.


Storage & Organization:


	Ensure proper storage conditions in line with pharmaceutical standards (temperature, humidity, light control).
	Arrange products systematically for easy identification and retrieval.
	Maintain cleanliness, safety, and organization of the store.
	Monitor cold chain products (if applicable) and maintain temperature logs.


Stock Issuance & Distribution:


	Issue stock to pharmacy units/branches based on approved requisitions.
	Ensure proper documentation for all stock movements.
	Track inter-branch transfers and returns accurately.


Expiry & Loss Management:


	Monitor expiry dates regularly and implement strategies to minimize losses.
	Prepare reports on near-expiry and expired items.
	Coordinate removal and proper disposal of expired or damaged goods.
	Investigate causes of stock variances, theft, or losses.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Maintain up-to-date inventory records (manual and electronic systems).
	Generate regular reports (stock levels, consumption trends, variances, expiries).
	Assist in audit processes and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements (e.g., PCN guidelines).


Compliance & Quality Assurance:


	Ensure adherence to pharmaceutical regulations and internal SOPs.
	Support regulatory inspections and audits.
	Maintain proper documentation for traceability of all products.


Coordination & Communication:


	Work closely with pharmacists, procurement team, and management.
	Provide timely updates on stock status and reorder needs.
	Support forecasting and demand planning activities.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy of inventory records
	Reduction in stock variances
	Minimal expiry and wastage levels
	Timely stock replenishment
	Compliance with audit and regulatory standards


Required Qualification
Educational Qualification:
Minimum of HND/B.Sc. in:


	Pharmacy Technician Studies
	Pharmacology
	Biochemistry
	Supply Chain Management
	Business Administration or related field


Professional Requirements:


	Certification in Inventory Management or Supply Chain (added advantage)
	Knowledge of pharmaceutical products and storage requirements
	Familiarity with PCN regulations and guidelines (Nigeria)


Experience:


	
	Minimum of 2–4 years of experience in inventory/store management (preferably in a pharmacy or hospital setting)
	


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong attention to detail and accuracy
	Good knowledge of inventory software (e.g., Excel, ERP systems)
	Analytical and problem-solving skills
	Organizational and time management skills
	Integrity and accountability
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Additional Requirements:


	High level of honesty and trustworthiness
	Ability to handle confidential information
	Willingness to learn and adapt to new systems.


Working Conditions


	Work involves standing, lifting, and handling stock items
	May require extended hours during stock-taking or audits.


Application Closing Date: 30th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Pharmacist

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Description


	This is a full-time on-site role located in Abuja.
	The position involves managing and overseeing day-to-day pharmacy operations, including dispensing medications, providing patient consultations, managing inventory, and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining high standards of customer service and collaborating with healthcare professionals to optimize patient care.


Qualifications


	Valid license to practice the profession
	Pharmacy management, Inventory management, and Regulatory compliance experience
	Patient consultation and Customer service skills
	Strong organizational, Communication, and Interpersonal skills
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	Experience in the pharmaceutical industry and a degree in Pharmacy or a related field
	Proficiency in using pharmacy management software and other relevant tools
	Attention to detail and strong problem-solving skills
	Integrity and compassion.


Application Closing Date: 24th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: General Manager&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;

Description


	We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic General Manager to lead operations in our paint manufacturing company.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the business, including production, sales, finance, and administration, ensuring efficiency, profitability, and sustainable growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operations of the manufacturing plant and business units
	Develop and implement business strategies to achieve company goals
	Drive production efficiency, quality control, and cost optimization
	Supervise and coordinate departments including production, sales, finance, and HR
	Ensure compliance with regulatory standards and industry requirements
	Monitor financial performance, budgets, and profitability
	Lead business development and market expansion initiatives
	Establish and maintain strong relationships with clients, distributors, and stakeholders
	Ensure optimal utilization of resources (human, material, and financial)
	Implement policies and procedures to improve operational efficiency
	Provide leadership, direction, and performance management for staff.


Educational Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Chemical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Business Administration, or other related fields.
	MBA, Master&#39;s Degree or relevant postgraduate qualification is an added advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong leadership and organizational skills.
	Excellent business and financial acumen
	In-depth knowledge of manufacturing processes and operations
	Strategic thinking and decision-making ability
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Effective communication and negotiation skills
	Ability to drive growth and operational excellence.


Experience:


	10&ndash;15 years experience in manufacturing or industrial operations
	At least 5 years in a senior management or leadership role
	Experience in paint, coatings, or chemical manufacturing is highly preferred.


Health, Safety &amp; Compliance:


	Strong knowledge of HSE standards and industrial safety practices
	Familiarity with Nigerian regulatory requirements (e.g., SON, NESREA)
	Commitment to maintaining a safe and compliant work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;adkangroupjobs@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

General Manager Job at Adkan Group and Services Limited




        
    

        
        Adkan Services Nigeria Limited - Our client in the Paint Industry, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager 

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic General Manager to lead operations in our paint manufacturing company.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the business, including production, sales, finance, and administration, ensuring efficiency, profitability, and sustainable growth.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee daily operations of the manufacturing plant and business units
	Develop and implement business strategies to achieve company goals
	Drive production efficiency, quality control, and cost optimization
	Supervise and coordinate departments including production, sales, finance, and HR
	Ensure compliance with regulatory standards and industry requirements
	Monitor financial performance, budgets, and profitability
	Lead business development and market expansion initiatives
	Establish and maintain strong relationships with clients, distributors, and stakeholders
	Ensure optimal utilization of resources (human, material, and financial)
	Implement policies and procedures to improve operational efficiency
	Provide leadership, direction, and performance management for staff.


Educational Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Chemical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Business Administration, or other related fields.
	MBA, Master's Degree or relevant postgraduate qualification is an added advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong leadership and organizational skills.
	Excellent business and financial acumen
	In-depth knowledge of manufacturing processes and operations
	Strategic thinking and decision-making ability
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Effective communication and negotiation skills
	Ability to drive growth and operational excellence.


Experience:


	10–15 years experience in manufacturing or industrial operations
	At least 5 years in a senior management or leadership role
	Experience in paint, coatings, or chemical manufacturing is highly preferred.


Health, Safety & Compliance:


	Strong knowledge of HSE standards and industrial safety practices
	Familiarity with Nigerian regulatory requirements (e.g., SON, NESREA)
	Commitment to maintaining a safe and compliant work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "description": "Iboj Consult is a recruiting and consulting firm that specializes in identifying and attracting top talent for companies across various industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a results-driven Sales Executive with proven experience in the automobile industry, specifically in spare parts, warranty management, and dealership operations.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving sales growth, managing customer relationships, and ensuring excellent service delivery across all touchpoints.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute sales strategies to achieve revenue targets for vehicles and spare parts.
	Manage end-to-end sales processes within dealership operations.
	Promote and sell automobile spare parts to retail and wholesale customers.
	Handle warranty claims, ensuring proper documentation and timely processing.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers, vendors, and partners.
	Provide product knowledge and technical guidance to customers.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and customer needs.
	Coordinate with service and technical teams to ensure customer satisfaction.
	Maintain accurate sales records, reports, and forecasts.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and dealership standards.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	BSC/HND in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field (preferred).
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in automobile sales.
	Strong experience in spare parts sales, warranty processes, and dealership operations.
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong customer service orientation.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;ibojconsult@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Sales Executive Job at Iboj Consult




        
    

        
        Iboj Consult is a recruiting and consulting firm that specializes in identifying and attracting top talent for companies across various industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a results-driven Sales Executive with proven experience in the automobile industry, specifically in spare parts, warranty management, and dealership operations.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving sales growth, managing customer relationships, and ensuring excellent service delivery across all touchpoints.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute sales strategies to achieve revenue targets for vehicles and spare parts.
	Manage end-to-end sales processes within dealership operations.
	Promote and sell automobile spare parts to retail and wholesale customers.
	Handle warranty claims, ensuring proper documentation and timely processing.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers, vendors, and partners.
	Provide product knowledge and technical guidance to customers.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and customer needs.
	Coordinate with service and technical teams to ensure customer satisfaction.
	Maintain accurate sales records, reports, and forecasts.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and dealership standards.


Requirements & Qualifications


	BSC/HND in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field (preferred).
	3–5 years of experience in automobile sales.
	Strong experience in spare parts sales, warranty processes, and dealership operations.
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong customer service orientation.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales & Marketing Lead",
  "description": "Imperial Homes Mortgage Bank Limited is a Mortgage Bank duly licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria to undertake mortgage banking and related services. It was originally incorporated in 1992 as Citizens Savings and Loans Limited. But the name was changed in 1997 to New Patriot Building Society Limited. Guaranty Trust Bank plc acquired the company in August 2007 as a wholly-owned subsidiary and changed its name to GTHomes Limited to reflect its relationship with GTBank. Imperial Homes Mortgage Bank Limited then commenced operation in January 2008.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales &amp; Marketing Lead

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Drive the marketing and sales functions &ndash; targets Nigerians locally and in Diaspora
	Generate leads, build and nurture client relationships, promote and sell company&rsquo;s products to target markets to meet their needs and achieve sales targets.
	On-board and manage Realtors, Agents and Brokers selling for the company.
	Prepare monthly project reports and send same to all subscribers on all Projects.
	Manage and monitor payments schedule and installments.
	Responsible for Marketing materials and sales events to drive visibility.
	Provide high-level reports and updates to Management.


Competency / Skill Requirements


	Knowledge and understanding of the Nigerian Lagos real estate market.
	Good product development and portfolio management capabilities.
	Excellent customer relationship development/management skills.
	Must have a high sense of responsibility, accountability, integrity and ethical standards.
	Must have strong business acumen, maturity and tact, including the ability to relate with customers, partners and other internal and external stakeholders.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;oluwafemi.awoyinfa@imperialmortgagebank.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Sales & Marketing Lead Job at Imperial Homes Mortgage Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Imperial Homes Mortgage Bank Limited is a Mortgage Bank duly licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria to undertake mortgage banking and related services. It was originally incorporated in 1992 as Citizens Savings and Loans Limited. But the name was changed in 1997 to New Patriot Building Society Limited. Guaranty Trust Bank plc acquired the company in August 2007 as a wholly-owned subsidiary and changed its name to GTHomes Limited to reflect its relationship with GTBank. Imperial Homes Mortgage Bank Limited then commenced operation in January 2008.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales & Marketing Lead

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Responsibilities


	Drive the marketing and sales functions – targets Nigerians locally and in Diaspora
	Generate leads, build and nurture client relationships, promote and sell company’s products to target markets to meet their needs and achieve sales targets.
	On-board and manage Realtors, Agents and Brokers selling for the company.
	Prepare monthly project reports and send same to all subscribers on all Projects.
	Manage and monitor payments schedule and installments.
	Responsible for Marketing materials and sales events to drive visibility.
	Provide high-level reports and updates to Management.


Competency / Skill Requirements


	Knowledge and understanding of the Nigerian Lagos real estate market.
	Good product development and portfolio management capabilities.
	Excellent customer relationship development/management skills.
	Must have a high sense of responsibility, accountability, integrity and ethical standards.
	Must have strong business acumen, maturity and tact, including the ability to relate with customers, partners and other internal and external stakeholders.


Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive (Salary Loan / Wealth Management)",
  "description": "Rosabon Financial Services (RFS) Limited - Award winning Rosabon Financial Services is a member of the Concept Group. Licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria, CBN on April 22, 1993 and being a long standing member of the Equipment Leasing Association of Nigeria (ELAN), the organization has evolved into Nigeria&rsquo;s leading Non-Banking Financial Intermediary and Equipment Leasing firm focused on niche and general markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive (Salary Loan / Wealth Management)

Job Location:&nbsp;Osogbo, Osun
Employment Type: Full-time

Scope and Impact


	The candidate will be tasked with the responsibility of selling various loan products.
	He/ She will act as liaison between customers and our financial institution and will help qualified applicants acquire loans in a timely manner.


Job Summary


	The Rosabon Sales Executive is required to collect and prepare all materials needed to process transactions (loans and investments).
	He/ She will also responsible for reviewing loan applications evaluating the potential risks of granting loans to applicants


Duties and Responsibilities


	Evaluate credit worthiness by processing loan applications and documentation within specified limits
	Interview applicants to determine financial eligibility and feasibility of granting loans
	Determine all applicable ratios and metrics and set up debt payment plans
	Communicate with clients either to request or to provide information
	Justify decisions (approvals/rejections) and report on them
	Complete loan contracts and counsel clients on policies and restrictions
	Review loan agreements to ensure that they are complete and accurate according to policy
	Update account records, Compute payment schedules and transactions on ERP
	Confer with underwriters to aid in resolving loan application problems.
	Update job knowledge on types of loans and other financial services
	Assess customer needs, explore all options and introduce different types of loans
	Develop referral networks, suggest alternate channels and cross-sell products and services to accomplish quotas
	Go the &ldquo;extra mile&rdquo; to build trust relationships, customer loyalty and satisfaction throughout the underwriting process
	Operate in compliance with laws and regulations and adhere to lending compliance guidelines
	Deposit mobilization to meet set targets.


Key Performance Indicators


	Meet Set Monthly loan and investment Targets
	Reduced number of Non-Performing loans
	Customer retention rate of minimum 90% across loan and investment products.


Requirements


	BSc Degree in Finance, Economics or a related field
	Proven working experience as a Loan Officer
	Familiarity with computers and banking applications/software
	Solid understanding of direct/indirect lending products and practices
	Customer satisfaction orientation and sales competencies
	Ability to work in a goal oriented environment and meet set target.


Job Knowledge:


	Solid understanding of local, state, and federal loan regulations.
	Knowledge of various financial products across the industry and sector
	Customer service oriented with exceptional Skills/Competencies
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Integrity and Analytical skills.
	Strong Negotiation and sales skills
	Proficient in MS packages and Software
	In-depth knowledge of computers, MS Packages and banking software.


Salary
N80,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should:&nbsp;Click here to apply online
Or
Send their Applications to:&nbsp;samson.adewuyi@conceptgroup-ng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  },

Sales Executive (Salary Loan / Wealth Management) Job at Rosabon Financial Services




        
    

        
        Rosabon Financial Services (RFS) Limited - Award winning Rosabon Financial Services is a member of the Concept Group. Licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria, CBN on April 22, 1993 and being a long standing member of the Equipment Leasing Association of Nigeria (ELAN), the organization has evolved into Nigeria’s leading Non-Banking Financial Intermediary and Equipment Leasing firm focused on niche and general markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive (Salary Loan / Wealth Management)

Job Location: Osogbo, Osun
Employment Type: Full-time

Scope and Impact
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The candidate will be tasked with the responsibility of selling various loan products.
	He/ She will act as liaison between customers and our financial institution and will help qualified applicants acquire loans in a timely manner.


Job Summary


	The Rosabon Sales Executive is required to collect and prepare all materials needed to process transactions (loans and investments).
	He/ She will also responsible for reviewing loan applications evaluating the potential risks of granting loans to applicants


Duties and Responsibilities


	Evaluate credit worthiness by processing loan applications and documentation within specified limits
	Interview applicants to determine financial eligibility and feasibility of granting loans
	Determine all applicable ratios and metrics and set up debt payment plans
	Communicate with clients either to request or to provide information
	Justify decisions (approvals/rejections) and report on them
	Complete loan contracts and counsel clients on policies and restrictions
	Review loan agreements to ensure that they are complete and accurate according to policy
	Update account records, Compute payment schedules and transactions on ERP
	Confer with underwriters to aid in resolving loan application problems.
	Update job knowledge on types of loans and other financial services
	Assess customer needs, explore all options and introduce different types of loans
	Develop referral networks, suggest alternate channels and cross-sell products and services to accomplish quotas
	Go the “extra mile” to build trust relationships, customer loyalty and satisfaction throughout the underwriting process
	Operate in compliance with laws and regulations and adhere to lending compliance guidelines
	Deposit mobilization to meet set targets.


Key Performance Indicators


	Meet Set Monthly loan and investment Targets
	Reduced number of Non-Performing loans
	Customer retention rate of minimum 90% across loan and investment products.


Requirements


	BSc Degree in Finance, Economics or a related field
	Proven working experience as a Loan Officer
	Familiarity with computers and banking applications/software
	Solid understanding of direct/indirect lending products and practices
	Customer satisfaction orientation and sales competencies
	Ability to work in a goal oriented environment and meet set target.


Job Knowledge:


	Solid understanding of local, state, and federal loan regulations.
	Knowledge of various financial products across the industry and sector
	Customer service oriented with exceptional Skills/Competencies
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Integrity and Analytical skills.
	Strong Negotiation and sales skills
	Proficient in MS packages and Software
	In-depth knowledge of computers, MS Packages and banking software.


Salary
N80,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application

Or
Send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
